channel.title: 大狸子切切裡 Cherry OFFICIAL CHANNEL[YT+]

image.title: 大狸子切切裡 Cherry OFFICIAL CHANNEL[YT+]

channel.copyright: 大狸子切切裡 Cherry OFFICIAL CHANNEL[YT+]
channel.itunes:author: 大狸子切切裡 Cherry OFFICIAL CHANNEL[YT+]

itunes:owner.itunes:name: 大狸子切切裡 Cherry OFFICIAL CHANNEL[YT+]

item.title: 日本線下竟然可以買到這個？！體驗碾壓網購？｜大狸子切切裡
item.itunes:title: 日本線下竟然可以買到這個？！體驗碾壓網購？｜大狸子切切裡
item.itunes:author: 大狸子切切裡 Cherry OFFICIAL CHANNEL
item.itunes:summary:
值得閱讀的理由

  • 深入了解日本線下購物的真實面貌，挑戰其是否真的比線上購物更發達的刻板印象。
  • 透過作者的實地探訪，一窺日本大型電器行（如 Bic Camera）在商品多樣性和購物體驗上的獨特之處。
  • 發現日本零售市場中令人意想不到的商品種類，從古老手機、互動式展示到充滿特色的利基型商店。

摘要

啟程：驗證日本線下購物神話

影片的作者對長久以來聽聞的日本線下購物比線上購物更發達的說法感到好奇。儘管日本早有線上購物平台，但在2025年的今天，為何仍存在這種觀點？是真實情況，還是我們對日本的刻板印象？為了釐清真相，作者決定親自從深圳飛往東京，展開一場探訪之旅，並設定了多項挑戰項目，首要便是驗證日本線下購物的商品多樣性是否真的媲美甚至超越線上購物。

挑戰：線下購物的商品多樣性

抵達日本的第二天，作者來到新宿的 Bic Camera，這是一家大型連鎖百貨，販售日用品、服飾、電器等綜合性商品。作者在此挑戰線下購物的商品是否齊全。在電器區域，作者發現從手機殼、網路線到平板保護貼，商品種類極為豐富。特別是保護貼區域，提供了可供直接觸摸甚至試寫的展示樣品，這種互動式體驗是線上購物難以比擬的。作者認為，就商品多樣性而言，這裡完全不輸給線上購物平台，商品種類非常齊全。

挑戰：尋找特定商品與老派裝置

在 Bic Camera 的手機區，作者看到了 OPPO、小米、華為等品牌櫃位，販售的機型更新速度尚可，甚至有最新的旗艦機。然而，他也發現了仍販售帶有實體導航鍵的古老手機，這與線上流傳的日本仍有許多老舊或低配高價手機的說法似乎有所印證。隨後，作者嘗試尋找一個相對不常見的特定商品——桌面吸塵器，雖然店員熱心協助尋找，最終只找到類似款，這顯示要線下找到一模一樣的商品可能較難。此外，作者還遇到部分商品僅提供一次性購買且無保固的情況。

深入探索：特色店家與商品

旅程的第三天，作者探訪了一家非常特別的店家，樓下販售復古遊戲機，樓上則是成人相關商品。作者描述了成人區依公司和女優分類，並解釋了日本法律對「猥褻物」的定義如何透過打碼來迴避，形成一種法律的灰色地帶。他還展示了成人影片的實體光碟封面以及令人驚訝的名人同人誌周邊。回到樓下，作者展示了許多復古遊戲機，如 GBA、Game Boy、Wii、GameCube 等，並注意到它們的價格差異很大，有的甚至已是損壞的「垃圾」狀態，但也充滿收藏價值。

================================================

Put the money directly into the machine. and it will pop out the corresponding steel balls. Why are there still the three major physical keys? There are even settings collections for these games available for purchase. Hi hi hi. Today's video is a little different from usual. Because the place I am at now is Japan. I've always heard before. that offline shopping in Japan is very developed. It even beats online shopping. This makes me very curious. As early as 1997,. Japan had its first online shopping platform,. Rakuten Market. But it's already 2025 now. Why is the online shopping experience in Japan. not as good as offline shopping? Or is this. just our stereotype? To figure these out,. I decided to fly directly from Shenzhen to Tokyo. Let's explore the differences together. Huh? Ah, that's terrifying. The Happy Valley effect has kicked in, guys. What on earth is this thing? It's so scary. I'm scared now. Wow, this room. Isn't it. a huge difference from the picture. when we booked the hotel? My goodness. This room is too small. Let's check out the bathroom. In Japan, even in such a small space,. they still have to have a bathtub. This time,. I've set up some challenge items on purpose. I want to see if the legendary offline shopping. is really that amazing. For example, the variety of products. Usually online,. we can buy almost anything. But in Japan offline,. can shopping be like that too? Hey, today is our second day in Japan. The place I'm at now. is in front of Bic Camera in Shinjuku. This should be considered. one of the largest chain department stores in Japan. It's a bit like the large-scale comprehensive commercial complexs. in our country more than ten years ago. There are daily necessities,. clothing,. electrical appliances and so on. Today we are going to challenge. and see whether the variety of products here is complete. and what the experience of offline shopping is really like. Then let's set off directly now. BIG BIG BIG BIG CAMERA. Here you can see some mall directories. It's a bit like the contents. that those tag jumps to. when we shop online. As soon as I came down, I saw a whole wall of phone cases.

But if you take a close look,. it's actually quite interesting. These are still relatively normal. What do you feel about all these? It seems that Japanese really like these flip phone cases. Is it a bit of a stereotype? Wow, I see something super interesting. Usually, we just throw these covers away after taking things apart,. but they actually sell them here. You could buy one and lose it every day. A row of Ethernet cables is laid out here for you. You can buy according to your own needs. Ha ha. 50 meters. That's a new record. I think this is even cooler than JD's window. If you don't recognize the head of this cable,. it doesn't matter. You can just bring your own cable over,. and it'll tell you what kind of port it is here. For example, this is Micro-B. And this is mini-B. Now the salesclerk is showing us the way. Thank you. These are tablet films. So many. A whole shelf full. And it's really good. They just show it to you like this. You can see it clearly. Let's take a look. I brought a screen with me. I can just stick it on like this. to see if it affects the clarity. If you try something like a paper-like film,. you can even bring your own pen. and draw on it. See if it's OK or not. I was just about to say that this is what the sample looks like,. or maybe this is just how the product packaging is. Because if you touch it directly, won't it wear out? Look, it seems like the clerk cut this part themselves. There are even knife marks on it. This is so much fun. There's also a price for the screen protector here. For screens above 12.1 inches, it's 6000 yen. That's 300 RMB (about 40 dollars). I'd rather earn that money myself. As for pens,. it's quite interesting here, too. There's a whole bunch of different kinds. What do you call this one? It feels like something from 10 years ago. Yeah, it looks just like a product from 10 years ago. A fabric head. What is this? I've seen it before,. but I've never actually bought it. And I found something really funny. I can actually take this pen over here,. and write directly on it. Well,. the texture is quite amazing. Can you guys feel through my camera lens. that there are too many things to see them all here?

It's overwhelming. But when it comes to the variety of products,. it's not inferior to the online experience at all. It's really, really comprehensive. BIG BIG CAMERA. I'm currently at the mobile phone counter,. and I see sections for OPPO and Xiaomi. Both of these brands have flagship models for sale,. and the update speed isn't particularly slow. For example, the Xiaomi 15 Ultra. came out around March,. and it's already available here at the counter. Take a look at this price. For the 1T Xiaomi,. it costs about 1400 dollars. OPPO's updates are a bit slower. This one here is the OPPO Find X8. I want to see if they have the OPPO Find X8 Ultra,. but it seems they don't yet. Actually, I've seen a lot online before. about how Japan has a lot of vintage phones,. or low-config, high-priced phones. that are still on sale. This time, I specifically looked for them,. and you know what? I found something interesting. Ah,. What is this vintage machine? Why does it still have the three major physical keys? Phone, home, information. Ah. Could it be the fingerprint unlock button? This one here is the voice button. I didn't expect to find. such a large Huawei counter in Japan. And the variety is pretty rich. Except for not having phones,. they have watches, tablets,. and I even saw glasses. Why do they even have purchase bonuses? So, if at this moment,. I want to buy some trendy items,. or products. that are less commonly used,. how will the experience be? I just found a desktop vacuum cleaner,. which is relatively rare. but not something extremely hard to find. We'll ask the clerk later. if there are any identical or similar products. available for purchase. Excuse me. I'm looking for this,. desktop vacuum cleaner. Okay. Thank you. The lady just now. really helped me find the same kind of desktop cleaner,. though it's not exactly the same model. You can only find similar products here. So,. if you really want to the find the exact same one,. it's rather difficult. You can only find similar ones offline. As for this machine,.

the lady just now spent a long time looking it up,. typing for a while,. and she told me this product is a one-time payment,. with no warranty. If I take it home and find it's broken,. there's nothing I can do about it. This looks a bit spicy. Excuse me, when do you update? This place here. is the famous spot from "Suzume no Tojimari," Ochanomizu. On the internet,. there are many guides. that tell you at certain time,. you might be able to see three trains in one frame. Actually, I didn't wait for it this time. So I used a bit of magic. How about it? Do you like it? Today is actually just the first part of the itinerary,. but everyone has been shopping all day,. and we're all a bit tired. So I brought my friends here for a nice meal. The average cost per person for this meal is about 70 dollars. So expensive. Guess how much this cup of cola costs? 660 yen (about 4 dollars). Hey! Today is our third day in Japan,. and we specifically find a. very, very special store. Downstairs, they sell some vintage game consoles,. but upstairs, there are some adult-oriented products. Let's go check it out together. As for the stuff downstairs,. I guess you guys probably wouldn't be interested in that. so I'm gonna blur them out. But I'll give you a quick description. The adult section here is split into two floors. The upper floor is for live-action adult movies,. and this floor here is all about adult anime or adult games. And actually, these games here. are pretty similar to the ones we usually play. Like, you've got games for PS3, PS4,. and they even sell these collector's editions,. you know, the boxed sets. with special goodies. It's kinda cool. Actually, not all the games here. are super explicit. Some are just a bit suggestive,. maybe not even reaching the 18+ level. My friend here spotted. something really abstract. I just looked it up,. and it's a game where they've turned all these characters from Romance of the Three Kingdoms, like Zhang Fei and all,. into these cute, moe-fied versions. It's so bizarre. So, the previous floor was. all about adult anime and games. Now, this floor is all about. live-action adult movies. And the way they categorize the shelves here is. by company names. and the artists under those companies. But speaking of the adult film industry in Japan,. there's something really counterintuitive about it.

In Japanese law,. publicly displaying obscene materials is actually. considered a crime. So,. technically, selling these adult movies should be illegal. But in practice,. as long as you blur out the sensitive parts,. magically, they're not considered. pornographic anymore. It's like a result of all the social negotiations and compromises. within Japan's legal gray areas. Another interesting thing is,. when we usually download "study materials," there's always a cover image, right? Well, here,. that's what the physical disc covers look like. But of course, the actual product might vary. And then, here are some accessories. The cabinet up front is full of regular... uh, "cups." But look at this row in the back. It's amazing! These are all fan-made works featuring celebrities. Alright, we've had our fun exploring the interesting stuff. And now. let's check out something we can actually show you guys. Let me take you in and see what they've got here. There are vintage consoles,. GBA. and even a Game Boy. But take a look at these prices. There's an old Wii here too,. and some without the original box and manuals. This one here probably doesn't even turn on anymore. Oh, look at this GameCube. I kind of want to take one home,. even though it's basically junk. And there are so many game cartridges over here. Wow, there's a huge pile. In this basket,. there's a bunch of the oldest generation PS3s,. just tossed in here like trash. And you can see these PSPs. without the original boxes. They're just in these plastic cases,. which is actually pretty good. It keeps them from getting damaged. when people rummage through them. And another perk of buying second-hand in person. is that I can see the actual condition of the console. You never know. if the seller's idea of "90% new" matches yours. For example, what do you think this one is? "99% new, used by a girl." Hahaha. And over here,. these are the PS2s, the best-selling game consoles in the world. But let's take a look at these prices. They're definitely not for the faint of heart. These tags here. are like the descriptions you see on second-hand platforms,. saying things like "slightly flawed" or "joystick drift." Oh,. what's this?

Wow, such a cover is so classic. Rathalos and Nargacuga,. I gotta take this one home. Walking by here, I spotted a pachinko parlor. It's a pretty famous form of entertainment in Japan,. and I just happened to pass by today. Let's go in and see how it's played. But a word of sincere advice to all of you: don't gamble. Stepping into the store,. the first thing that hits you is how incredibly noisy it is. Actually, playing pachinko is pretty straightforward. You just put money into the machine,. and it spits out corresponding steel balls. You twist this knob here,. and the ball shoots out from one side of the machine. The harder you twist, the farther the ball goes. If the ball lands in the middle slot,. you win. In reality, while playing,. you're just holding onto this knob the whole time,. keeping it steady. There's no real skill or strategy involved. When you're done,. you can take all your remaining steel balls. to the front counter and exchange them for prizes. Here's the interesting part:. gambling is illegal in Japan,. so these pachinko parlors. can never exchange your steel balls. for cash. Instead,. they give you souvenirs like this. A silver token,. which isn't worth much on its own. But around the shop,. maybe within a 50-meter radius,. you can sell it for way more than its actual value. Strictly speaking,. the pachinko parlor isn't really running a gambling operation. They're just selling you a regular souvenir. But since this souvenir can be easily exchanged for cash,. everyone knows deep down that. it's essentially gambling. On the last night,. I decided to check out the Monster Hunter tavern up in Akihabara. There's actually an FF café. in the same building too. I'm still holding onto these cards I got here the year before last. Let's head upstairs. Ding ding. Here we are. This is a Stamp Card. I got two years ago. If you collect 10 stamps,. you can redeem an acrylic stand like this one. If one stamp only gets you a small card like this,. I'd definitely save up more stamps. First off, everyone gets a blind - box item. What kind of dragon you'll get on this coaster? It's all up to random chance. This manual here. is called. "The Beginner's Guide to the Monster Hunter Bar." And here's. the interesting part:. when you check out, they charge each person a table fee,.

but in return,. you get a daily login reward. Just like in the game,. where you get a reward for logging in every day. To sum up my experience at this Monster Hunter tavern today,. even if you bring a friend who doesn't play Monster Hunter,. it's still an absolutely fantastic time. The atmosphere is amazing. The bar's decor and the dishes they serve. all get updated when a new game in the series is released. This is also the first time I've ordered so many dishes here. Well, I guess you're mostly paying for. the names and the cool designs of the food. The prices aren't outrageously overpriced because of the IP,. but they're still a bit on the pricey side. When "Monster Hunter: World" came out,. I went to the Shinjuku branch. Then, for "Rise" and now for "Wilds," I've come to the Akihabara store. As soon as you walk in, there's a Palico greeting you at the door. Inside, you can see the quests. on the quest board. There's a Rathalos greatsword hanging on the wall,. an Iceborne bow,. and up in the sky, there's a representation of a Yian Kut - Ku hunting ground. It just feels like the Yian Kut - Ku is about to charge and spit fireballs at you. After my visit five years ago,. I was so inspired that. I started buying 1:1 scale weapons from the game. to decorate my studio. So, this trip to Japan has come to an end. But this time, I approached it with an observational mindset,. and it allowed me to discover many things. that I hadn't noticed during previous travels. Before setting off,. I was actually curious about. why Japan always gives us this stereotype of. being stuck in the past. Well, after coming to Tokyo this time,. I've got some new thoughts. More than one-tenth of Japan's population is in Tokyo. After long-term development,. there are so many different rules here. Everyone tries their best to go about their business without disturbing others,. following the most reliable procedures. Nowadays, in Japanese ramen shops,. many still use coin-operated ticket vending machines. And a lot of companies are still using fax machines. But perhaps it's because there are just too many constraints. that there's a lack of fertile ground for something new and innovative to emerge. In the past, I used to think that. if I were a tech and gadget blogger,. I'd have to travel abroad frequently. since the latest and greatest products are always released overseas. But in recent years,. I've noticed that more and more fun stuff is being launched in China first. At that point,. actually, many friends from all over the world. come to watch our videos. to get the latest news right away. That's why my YouTube videos. have always had meticulously proofread English subtitles. Sometimes, seeing the interactions from international viewers. is quite interesting. Oh, and here's something fun.

I've managed to secure my Schengen visa, UK visa, and US visa. So, if in the future, there's anything interesting. you guys want to see,. I can just fly over there and take you all along for the ride.

=============

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zfvLfK1HUPQ

歡迎訂閱切狸~ 在這裡你會看到很多有趣的數碼資訊哦！ | Please don’t forget to subscribe 💋：https://bit.ly/3jQ3429

-------------------------------------------------- ----------------------------------

其實對於日本，除了實體購物和網路購買的差別，我有很多好奇的地方，像是傳說中的日本古老的電腦、手機，是不是真的很多人在用？又例如國產的華米OV在國外的版本，跟國銀用起來有沒有什麼差別？如果大家想看的話，下次再帶大家來一次日本。除此之外，也可以再在評論或彈幕裡留下你們的建議，我之後可以直接飛過去帶大家一起康！

F O L L O W M E

Weibo: @大狸子切切里

Bilibili: https://space.bilibili.com/471303350

#數碼 #日本 #購物
item.description:
值得閱讀的理由

  • 深入了解日本線下購物的真實面貌，挑戰其是否真的比線上購物更發達的刻板印象。
  • 透過作者的實地探訪，一窺日本大型電器行（如 Bic Camera）在商品多樣性和購物體驗上的獨特之處。
  • 發現日本零售市場中令人意想不到的商品種類，從古老手機、互動式展示到充滿特色的利基型商店。

摘要

啟程：驗證日本線下購物神話

影片的作者對長久以來聽聞的日本線下購物比線上購物更發達的說法感到好奇。儘管日本早有線上購物平台，但在2025年的今天，為何仍存在這種觀點？是真實情況，還是我們對日本的刻板印象？為了釐清真相，作者決定親自從深圳飛往東京，展開一場探訪之旅，並設定了多項挑戰項目，首要便是驗證日本線下購物的商品多樣性是否真的媲美甚至超越線上購物。

挑戰：線下購物的商品多樣性

抵達日本的第二天，作者來到新宿的 Bic Camera，這是一家大型連鎖百貨，販售日用品、服飾、電器等綜合性商品。作者在此挑戰線下購物的商品是否齊全。在電器區域，作者發現從手機殼、網路線到平板保護貼，商品種類極為豐富。特別是保護貼區域，提供了可供直接觸摸甚至試寫的展示樣品，這種互動式體驗是線上購物難以比擬的。作者認為，就商品多樣性而言，這裡完全不輸給線上購物平台，商品種類非常齊全。

挑戰：尋找特定商品與老派裝置

在 Bic Camera 的手機區，作者看到了 OPPO、小米、華為等品牌櫃位，販售的機型更新速度尚可，甚至有最新的旗艦機。然而，他也發現了仍販售帶有實體導航鍵的古老手機，這與線上流傳的日本仍有許多老舊或低配高價手機的說法似乎有所印證。隨後，作者嘗試尋找一個相對不常見的特定商品——桌面吸塵器，雖然店員熱心協助尋找，最終只找到類似款，這顯示要線下找到一模一樣的商品可能較難。此外，作者還遇到部分商品僅提供一次性購買且無保固的情況。

深入探索：特色店家與商品

旅程的第三天，作者探訪了一家非常特別的店家，樓下販售復古遊戲機，樓上則是成人相關商品。作者描述了成人區依公司和女優分類，並解釋了日本法律對「猥褻物」的定義如何透過打碼來迴避，形成一種法律的灰色地帶。他還展示了成人影片的實體光碟封面以及令人驚訝的名人同人誌周邊。回到樓下，作者展示了許多復古遊戲機，如 GBA、Game Boy、Wii、GameCube 等，並注意到它們的價格差異很大，有的甚至已是損壞的「垃圾」狀態，但也充滿收藏價值。

================================================

Put the money directly into the machine. and it will pop out the corresponding steel balls. Why are there still the three major physical keys? There are even settings collections for these games available for purchase. Hi hi hi. Today's video is a little different from usual. Because the place I am at now is Japan. I've always heard before. that offline shopping in Japan is very developed. It even beats online shopping. This makes me very curious. As early as 1997,. Japan had its first online shopping platform,. Rakuten Market. But it's already 2025 now. Why is the online shopping experience in Japan. not as good as offline shopping? Or is this. just our stereotype? To figure these out,. I decided to fly directly from Shenzhen to Tokyo. Let's explore the differences together. Huh? Ah, that's terrifying. The Happy Valley effect has kicked in, guys. What on earth is this thing? It's so scary. I'm scared now. Wow, this room. Isn't it. a huge difference from the picture. when we booked the hotel? My goodness. This room is too small. Let's check out the bathroom. In Japan, even in such a small space,. they still have to have a bathtub. This time,. I've set up some challenge items on purpose. I want to see if the legendary offline shopping. is really that amazing. For example, the variety of products. Usually online,. we can buy almost anything. But in Japan offline,. can shopping be like that too? Hey, today is our second day in Japan. The place I'm at now. is in front of Bic Camera in Shinjuku. This should be considered. one of the largest chain department stores in Japan. It's a bit like the large-scale comprehensive commercial complexs. in our country more than ten years ago. There are daily necessities,. clothing,. electrical appliances and so on. Today we are going to challenge. and see whether the variety of products here is complete. and what the experience of offline shopping is really like. Then let's set off directly now. BIG BIG BIG BIG CAMERA. Here you can see some mall directories. It's a bit like the contents. that those tag jumps to. when we shop online. As soon as I came down, I saw a whole wall of phone cases.

But if you take a close look,. it's actually quite interesting. These are still relatively normal. What do you feel about all these? It seems that Japanese really like these flip phone cases. Is it a bit of a stereotype? Wow, I see something super interesting. Usually, we just throw these covers away after taking things apart,. but they actually sell them here. You could buy one and lose it every day. A row of Ethernet cables is laid out here for you. You can buy according to your own needs. Ha ha. 50 meters. That's a new record. I think this is even cooler than JD's window. If you don't recognize the head of this cable,. it doesn't matter. You can just bring your own cable over,. and it'll tell you what kind of port it is here. For example, this is Micro-B. And this is mini-B. Now the salesclerk is showing us the way. Thank you. These are tablet films. So many. A whole shelf full. And it's really good. They just show it to you like this. You can see it clearly. Let's take a look. I brought a screen with me. I can just stick it on like this. to see if it affects the clarity. If you try something like a paper-like film,. you can even bring your own pen. and draw on it. See if it's OK or not. I was just about to say that this is what the sample looks like,. or maybe this is just how the product packaging is. Because if you touch it directly, won't it wear out? Look, it seems like the clerk cut this part themselves. There are even knife marks on it. This is so much fun. There's also a price for the screen protector here. For screens above 12.1 inches, it's 6000 yen. That's 300 RMB (about 40 dollars). I'd rather earn that money myself. As for pens,. it's quite interesting here, too. There's a whole bunch of different kinds. What do you call this one? It feels like something from 10 years ago. Yeah, it looks just like a product from 10 years ago. A fabric head. What is this? I've seen it before,. but I've never actually bought it. And I found something really funny. I can actually take this pen over here,. and write directly on it. Well,. the texture is quite amazing. Can you guys feel through my camera lens. that there are too many things to see them all here?

It's overwhelming. But when it comes to the variety of products,. it's not inferior to the online experience at all. It's really, really comprehensive. BIG BIG CAMERA. I'm currently at the mobile phone counter,. and I see sections for OPPO and Xiaomi. Both of these brands have flagship models for sale,. and the update speed isn't particularly slow. For example, the Xiaomi 15 Ultra. came out around March,. and it's already available here at the counter. Take a look at this price. For the 1T Xiaomi,. it costs about 1400 dollars. OPPO's updates are a bit slower. This one here is the OPPO Find X8. I want to see if they have the OPPO Find X8 Ultra,. but it seems they don't yet. Actually, I've seen a lot online before. about how Japan has a lot of vintage phones,. or low-config, high-priced phones. that are still on sale. This time, I specifically looked for them,. and you know what? I found something interesting. Ah,. What is this vintage machine? Why does it still have the three major physical keys? Phone, home, information. Ah. Could it be the fingerprint unlock button? This one here is the voice button. I didn't expect to find. such a large Huawei counter in Japan. And the variety is pretty rich. Except for not having phones,. they have watches, tablets,. and I even saw glasses. Why do they even have purchase bonuses? So, if at this moment,. I want to buy some trendy items,. or products. that are less commonly used,. how will the experience be? I just found a desktop vacuum cleaner,. which is relatively rare. but not something extremely hard to find. We'll ask the clerk later. if there are any identical or similar products. available for purchase. Excuse me. I'm looking for this,. desktop vacuum cleaner. Okay. Thank you. The lady just now. really helped me find the same kind of desktop cleaner,. though it's not exactly the same model. You can only find similar products here. So,. if you really want to the find the exact same one,. it's rather difficult. You can only find similar ones offline. As for this machine,.

the lady just now spent a long time looking it up,. typing for a while,. and she told me this product is a one-time payment,. with no warranty. If I take it home and find it's broken,. there's nothing I can do about it. This looks a bit spicy. Excuse me, when do you update? This place here. is the famous spot from "Suzume no Tojimari," Ochanomizu. On the internet,. there are many guides. that tell you at certain time,. you might be able to see three trains in one frame. Actually, I didn't wait for it this time. So I used a bit of magic. How about it? Do you like it? Today is actually just the first part of the itinerary,. but everyone has been shopping all day,. and we're all a bit tired. So I brought my friends here for a nice meal. The average cost per person for this meal is about 70 dollars. So expensive. Guess how much this cup of cola costs? 660 yen (about 4 dollars). Hey! Today is our third day in Japan,. and we specifically find a. very, very special store. Downstairs, they sell some vintage game consoles,. but upstairs, there are some adult-oriented products. Let's go check it out together. As for the stuff downstairs,. I guess you guys probably wouldn't be interested in that. so I'm gonna blur them out. But I'll give you a quick description. The adult section here is split into two floors. The upper floor is for live-action adult movies,. and this floor here is all about adult anime or adult games. And actually, these games here. are pretty similar to the ones we usually play. Like, you've got games for PS3, PS4,. and they even sell these collector's editions,. you know, the boxed sets. with special goodies. It's kinda cool. Actually, not all the games here. are super explicit. Some are just a bit suggestive,. maybe not even reaching the 18+ level. My friend here spotted. something really abstract. I just looked it up,. and it's a game where they've turned all these characters from Romance of the Three Kingdoms, like Zhang Fei and all,. into these cute, moe-fied versions. It's so bizarre. So, the previous floor was. all about adult anime and games. Now, this floor is all about. live-action adult movies. And the way they categorize the shelves here is. by company names. and the artists under those companies. But speaking of the adult film industry in Japan,. there's something really counterintuitive about it.

In Japanese law,. publicly displaying obscene materials is actually. considered a crime. So,. technically, selling these adult movies should be illegal. But in practice,. as long as you blur out the sensitive parts,. magically, they're not considered. pornographic anymore. It's like a result of all the social negotiations and compromises. within Japan's legal gray areas. Another interesting thing is,. when we usually download "study materials," there's always a cover image, right? Well, here,. that's what the physical disc covers look like. But of course, the actual product might vary. And then, here are some accessories. The cabinet up front is full of regular... uh, "cups." But look at this row in the back. It's amazing! These are all fan-made works featuring celebrities. Alright, we've had our fun exploring the interesting stuff. And now. let's check out something we can actually show you guys. Let me take you in and see what they've got here. There are vintage consoles,. GBA. and even a Game Boy. But take a look at these prices. There's an old Wii here too,. and some without the original box and manuals. This one here probably doesn't even turn on anymore. Oh, look at this GameCube. I kind of want to take one home,. even though it's basically junk. And there are so many game cartridges over here. Wow, there's a huge pile. In this basket,. there's a bunch of the oldest generation PS3s,. just tossed in here like trash. And you can see these PSPs. without the original boxes. They're just in these plastic cases,. which is actually pretty good. It keeps them from getting damaged. when people rummage through them. And another perk of buying second-hand in person. is that I can see the actual condition of the console. You never know. if the seller's idea of "90% new" matches yours. For example, what do you think this one is? "99% new, used by a girl." Hahaha. And over here,. these are the PS2s, the best-selling game consoles in the world. But let's take a look at these prices. They're definitely not for the faint of heart. These tags here. are like the descriptions you see on second-hand platforms,. saying things like "slightly flawed" or "joystick drift." Oh,. what's this?

Wow, such a cover is so classic. Rathalos and Nargacuga,. I gotta take this one home. Walking by here, I spotted a pachinko parlor. It's a pretty famous form of entertainment in Japan,. and I just happened to pass by today. Let's go in and see how it's played. But a word of sincere advice to all of you: don't gamble. Stepping into the store,. the first thing that hits you is how incredibly noisy it is. Actually, playing pachinko is pretty straightforward. You just put money into the machine,. and it spits out corresponding steel balls. You twist this knob here,. and the ball shoots out from one side of the machine. The harder you twist, the farther the ball goes. If the ball lands in the middle slot,. you win. In reality, while playing,. you're just holding onto this knob the whole time,. keeping it steady. There's no real skill or strategy involved. When you're done,. you can take all your remaining steel balls. to the front counter and exchange them for prizes. Here's the interesting part:. gambling is illegal in Japan,. so these pachinko parlors. can never exchange your steel balls. for cash. Instead,. they give you souvenirs like this. A silver token,. which isn't worth much on its own. But around the shop,. maybe within a 50-meter radius,. you can sell it for way more than its actual value. Strictly speaking,. the pachinko parlor isn't really running a gambling operation. They're just selling you a regular souvenir. But since this souvenir can be easily exchanged for cash,. everyone knows deep down that. it's essentially gambling. On the last night,. I decided to check out the Monster Hunter tavern up in Akihabara. There's actually an FF café. in the same building too. I'm still holding onto these cards I got here the year before last. Let's head upstairs. Ding ding. Here we are. This is a Stamp Card. I got two years ago. If you collect 10 stamps,. you can redeem an acrylic stand like this one. If one stamp only gets you a small card like this,. I'd definitely save up more stamps. First off, everyone gets a blind - box item. What kind of dragon you'll get on this coaster? It's all up to random chance. This manual here. is called. "The Beginner's Guide to the Monster Hunter Bar." And here's. the interesting part:. when you check out, they charge each person a table fee,.

but in return,. you get a daily login reward. Just like in the game,. where you get a reward for logging in every day. To sum up my experience at this Monster Hunter tavern today,. even if you bring a friend who doesn't play Monster Hunter,. it's still an absolutely fantastic time. The atmosphere is amazing. The bar's decor and the dishes they serve. all get updated when a new game in the series is released. This is also the first time I've ordered so many dishes here. Well, I guess you're mostly paying for. the names and the cool designs of the food. The prices aren't outrageously overpriced because of the IP,. but they're still a bit on the pricey side. When "Monster Hunter: World" came out,. I went to the Shinjuku branch. Then, for "Rise" and now for "Wilds," I've come to the Akihabara store. As soon as you walk in, there's a Palico greeting you at the door. Inside, you can see the quests. on the quest board. There's a Rathalos greatsword hanging on the wall,. an Iceborne bow,. and up in the sky, there's a representation of a Yian Kut - Ku hunting ground. It just feels like the Yian Kut - Ku is about to charge and spit fireballs at you. After my visit five years ago,. I was so inspired that. I started buying 1:1 scale weapons from the game. to decorate my studio. So, this trip to Japan has come to an end. But this time, I approached it with an observational mindset,. and it allowed me to discover many things. that I hadn't noticed during previous travels. Before setting off,. I was actually curious about. why Japan always gives us this stereotype of. being stuck in the past. Well, after coming to Tokyo this time,. I've got some new thoughts. More than one-tenth of Japan's population is in Tokyo. After long-term development,. there are so many different rules here. Everyone tries their best to go about their business without disturbing others,. following the most reliable procedures. Nowadays, in Japanese ramen shops,. many still use coin-operated ticket vending machines. And a lot of companies are still using fax machines. But perhaps it's because there are just too many constraints. that there's a lack of fertile ground for something new and innovative to emerge. In the past, I used to think that. if I were a tech and gadget blogger,. I'd have to travel abroad frequently. since the latest and greatest products are always released overseas. But in recent years,. I've noticed that more and more fun stuff is being launched in China first. At that point,. actually, many friends from all over the world. come to watch our videos. to get the latest news right away. That's why my YouTube videos. have always had meticulously proofread English subtitles. Sometimes, seeing the interactions from international viewers. is quite interesting. Oh, and here's something fun.

I've managed to secure my Schengen visa, UK visa, and US visa. So, if in the future, there's anything interesting. you guys want to see,. I can just fly over there and take you all along for the ride.

=============

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zfvLfK1HUPQ

歡迎訂閱切狸~ 在這裡你會看到很多有趣的數碼資訊哦！ | Please don’t forget to subscribe 💋：https://bit.ly/3jQ3429

-------------------------------------------------- ----------------------------------

其實對於日本，除了實體購物和網路購買的差別，我有很多好奇的地方，像是傳說中的日本古老的電腦、手機，是不是真的很多人在用？又例如國產的華米OV在國外的版本，跟國銀用起來有沒有什麼差別？如果大家想看的話，下次再帶大家來一次日本。除此之外，也可以再在評論或彈幕裡留下你們的建議，我之後可以直接飛過去帶大家一起康！

F O L L O W M E

Weibo: @大狸子切切里

Bilibili: https://space.bilibili.com/471303350

#數碼 #日本 #購物
item.content:encoded:
值得閱讀的理由

  • 深入了解日本線下購物的真實面貌，挑戰其是否真的比線上購物更發達的刻板印象。
  • 透過作者的實地探訪，一窺日本大型電器行（如 Bic Camera）在商品多樣性和購物體驗上的獨特之處。
  • 發現日本零售市場中令人意想不到的商品種類，從古老手機、互動式展示到充滿特色的利基型商店。

摘要

啟程：驗證日本線下購物神話

影片的作者對長久以來聽聞的日本線下購物比線上購物更發達的說法感到好奇。儘管日本早有線上購物平台，但在2025年的今天，為何仍存在這種觀點？是真實情況，還是我們對日本的刻板印象？為了釐清真相，作者決定親自從深圳飛往東京，展開一場探訪之旅，並設定了多項挑戰項目，首要便是驗證日本線下購物的商品多樣性是否真的媲美甚至超越線上購物。

挑戰：線下購物的商品多樣性

抵達日本的第二天，作者來到新宿的 Bic Camera，這是一家大型連鎖百貨，販售日用品、服飾、電器等綜合性商品。作者在此挑戰線下購物的商品是否齊全。在電器區域，作者發現從手機殼、網路線到平板保護貼，商品種類極為豐富。特別是保護貼區域，提供了可供直接觸摸甚至試寫的展示樣品，這種互動式體驗是線上購物難以比擬的。作者認為，就商品多樣性而言，這裡完全不輸給線上購物平台，商品種類非常齊全。

挑戰：尋找特定商品與老派裝置

在 Bic Camera 的手機區，作者看到了 OPPO、小米、華為等品牌櫃位，販售的機型更新速度尚可，甚至有最新的旗艦機。然而，他也發現了仍販售帶有實體導航鍵的古老手機，這與線上流傳的日本仍有許多老舊或低配高價手機的說法似乎有所印證。隨後，作者嘗試尋找一個相對不常見的特定商品——桌面吸塵器，雖然店員熱心協助尋找，最終只找到類似款，這顯示要線下找到一模一樣的商品可能較難。此外，作者還遇到部分商品僅提供一次性購買且無保固的情況。

深入探索：特色店家與商品

旅程的第三天，作者探訪了一家非常特別的店家，樓下販售復古遊戲機，樓上則是成人相關商品。作者描述了成人區依公司和女優分類，並解釋了日本法律對「猥褻物」的定義如何透過打碼來迴避，形成一種法律的灰色地帶。他還展示了成人影片的實體光碟封面以及令人驚訝的名人同人誌周邊。回到樓下，作者展示了許多復古遊戲機，如 GBA、Game Boy、Wii、GameCube 等，並注意到它們的價格差異很大，有的甚至已是損壞的「垃圾」狀態，但也充滿收藏價值。

================================================

Put the money directly into the machine. and it will pop out the corresponding steel balls. Why are there still the three major physical keys? There are even settings collections for these games available for purchase. Hi hi hi. Today's video is a little different from usual. Because the place I am at now is Japan. I've always heard before. that offline shopping in Japan is very developed. It even beats online shopping. This makes me very curious. As early as 1997,. Japan had its first online shopping platform,. Rakuten Market. But it's already 2025 now. Why is the online shopping experience in Japan. not as good as offline shopping? Or is this. just our stereotype? To figure these out,. I decided to fly directly from Shenzhen to Tokyo. Let's explore the differences together. Huh? Ah, that's terrifying. The Happy Valley effect has kicked in, guys. What on earth is this thing? It's so scary. I'm scared now. Wow, this room. Isn't it. a huge difference from the picture. when we booked the hotel? My goodness. This room is too small. Let's check out the bathroom. In Japan, even in such a small space,. they still have to have a bathtub. This time,. I've set up some challenge items on purpose. I want to see if the legendary offline shopping. is really that amazing. For example, the variety of products. Usually online,. we can buy almost anything. But in Japan offline,. can shopping be like that too? Hey, today is our second day in Japan. The place I'm at now. is in front of Bic Camera in Shinjuku. This should be considered. one of the largest chain department stores in Japan. It's a bit like the large-scale comprehensive commercial complexs. in our country more than ten years ago. There are daily necessities,. clothing,. electrical appliances and so on. Today we are going to challenge. and see whether the variety of products here is complete. and what the experience of offline shopping is really like. Then let's set off directly now. BIG BIG BIG BIG CAMERA. Here you can see some mall directories. It's a bit like the contents. that those tag jumps to. when we shop online. As soon as I came down, I saw a whole wall of phone cases.

But if you take a close look,. it's actually quite interesting. These are still relatively normal. What do you feel about all these? It seems that Japanese really like these flip phone cases. Is it a bit of a stereotype? Wow, I see something super interesting. Usually, we just throw these covers away after taking things apart,. but they actually sell them here. You could buy one and lose it every day. A row of Ethernet cables is laid out here for you. You can buy according to your own needs. Ha ha. 50 meters. That's a new record. I think this is even cooler than JD's window. If you don't recognize the head of this cable,. it doesn't matter. You can just bring your own cable over,. and it'll tell you what kind of port it is here. For example, this is Micro-B. And this is mini-B. Now the salesclerk is showing us the way. Thank you. These are tablet films. So many. A whole shelf full. And it's really good. They just show it to you like this. You can see it clearly. Let's take a look. I brought a screen with me. I can just stick it on like this. to see if it affects the clarity. If you try something like a paper-like film,. you can even bring your own pen. and draw on it. See if it's OK or not. I was just about to say that this is what the sample looks like,. or maybe this is just how the product packaging is. Because if you touch it directly, won't it wear out? Look, it seems like the clerk cut this part themselves. There are even knife marks on it. This is so much fun. There's also a price for the screen protector here. For screens above 12.1 inches, it's 6000 yen. That's 300 RMB (about 40 dollars). I'd rather earn that money myself. As for pens,. it's quite interesting here, too. There's a whole bunch of different kinds. What do you call this one? It feels like something from 10 years ago. Yeah, it looks just like a product from 10 years ago. A fabric head. What is this? I've seen it before,. but I've never actually bought it. And I found something really funny. I can actually take this pen over here,. and write directly on it. Well,. the texture is quite amazing. Can you guys feel through my camera lens. that there are too many things to see them all here?

It's overwhelming. But when it comes to the variety of products,. it's not inferior to the online experience at all. It's really, really comprehensive. BIG BIG CAMERA. I'm currently at the mobile phone counter,. and I see sections for OPPO and Xiaomi. Both of these brands have flagship models for sale,. and the update speed isn't particularly slow. For example, the Xiaomi 15 Ultra. came out around March,. and it's already available here at the counter. Take a look at this price. For the 1T Xiaomi,. it costs about 1400 dollars. OPPO's updates are a bit slower. This one here is the OPPO Find X8. I want to see if they have the OPPO Find X8 Ultra,. but it seems they don't yet. Actually, I've seen a lot online before. about how Japan has a lot of vintage phones,. or low-config, high-priced phones. that are still on sale. This time, I specifically looked for them,. and you know what? I found something interesting. Ah,. What is this vintage machine? Why does it still have the three major physical keys? Phone, home, information. Ah. Could it be the fingerprint unlock button? This one here is the voice button. I didn't expect to find. such a large Huawei counter in Japan. And the variety is pretty rich. Except for not having phones,. they have watches, tablets,. and I even saw glasses. Why do they even have purchase bonuses? So, if at this moment,. I want to buy some trendy items,. or products. that are less commonly used,. how will the experience be? I just found a desktop vacuum cleaner,. which is relatively rare. but not something extremely hard to find. We'll ask the clerk later. if there are any identical or similar products. available for purchase. Excuse me. I'm looking for this,. desktop vacuum cleaner. Okay. Thank you. The lady just now. really helped me find the same kind of desktop cleaner,. though it's not exactly the same model. You can only find similar products here. So,. if you really want to the find the exact same one,. it's rather difficult. You can only find similar ones offline. As for this machine,.

the lady just now spent a long time looking it up,. typing for a while,. and she told me this product is a one-time payment,. with no warranty. If I take it home and find it's broken,. there's nothing I can do about it. This looks a bit spicy. Excuse me, when do you update? This place here. is the famous spot from "Suzume no Tojimari," Ochanomizu. On the internet,. there are many guides. that tell you at certain time,. you might be able to see three trains in one frame. Actually, I didn't wait for it this time. So I used a bit of magic. How about it? Do you like it? Today is actually just the first part of the itinerary,. but everyone has been shopping all day,. and we're all a bit tired. So I brought my friends here for a nice meal. The average cost per person for this meal is about 70 dollars. So expensive. Guess how much this cup of cola costs? 660 yen (about 4 dollars). Hey! Today is our third day in Japan,. and we specifically find a. very, very special store. Downstairs, they sell some vintage game consoles,. but upstairs, there are some adult-oriented products. Let's go check it out together. As for the stuff downstairs,. I guess you guys probably wouldn't be interested in that. so I'm gonna blur them out. But I'll give you a quick description. The adult section here is split into two floors. The upper floor is for live-action adult movies,. and this floor here is all about adult anime or adult games. And actually, these games here. are pretty similar to the ones we usually play. Like, you've got games for PS3, PS4,. and they even sell these collector's editions,. you know, the boxed sets. with special goodies. It's kinda cool. Actually, not all the games here. are super explicit. Some are just a bit suggestive,. maybe not even reaching the 18+ level. My friend here spotted. something really abstract. I just looked it up,. and it's a game where they've turned all these characters from Romance of the Three Kingdoms, like Zhang Fei and all,. into these cute, moe-fied versions. It's so bizarre. So, the previous floor was. all about adult anime and games. Now, this floor is all about. live-action adult movies. And the way they categorize the shelves here is. by company names. and the artists under those companies. But speaking of the adult film industry in Japan,. there's something really counterintuitive about it.

In Japanese law,. publicly displaying obscene materials is actually. considered a crime. So,. technically, selling these adult movies should be illegal. But in practice,. as long as you blur out the sensitive parts,. magically, they're not considered. pornographic anymore. It's like a result of all the social negotiations and compromises. within Japan's legal gray areas. Another interesting thing is,. when we usually download "study materials," there's always a cover image, right? Well, here,. that's what the physical disc covers look like. But of course, the actual product might vary. And then, here are some accessories. The cabinet up front is full of regular... uh, "cups." But look at this row in the back. It's amazing! These are all fan-made works featuring celebrities. Alright, we've had our fun exploring the interesting stuff. And now. let's check out something we can actually show you guys. Let me take you in and see what they've got here. There are vintage consoles,. GBA. and even a Game Boy. But take a look at these prices. There's an old Wii here too,. and some without the original box and manuals. This one here probably doesn't even turn on anymore. Oh, look at this GameCube. I kind of want to take one home,. even though it's basically junk. And there are so many game cartridges over here. Wow, there's a huge pile. In this basket,. there's a bunch of the oldest generation PS3s,. just tossed in here like trash. And you can see these PSPs. without the original boxes. They're just in these plastic cases,. which is actually pretty good. It keeps them from getting damaged. when people rummage through them. And another perk of buying second-hand in person. is that I can see the actual condition of the console. You never know. if the seller's idea of "90% new" matches yours. For example, what do you think this one is? "99% new, used by a girl." Hahaha. And over here,. these are the PS2s, the best-selling game consoles in the world. But let's take a look at these prices. They're definitely not for the faint of heart. These tags here. are like the descriptions you see on second-hand platforms,. saying things like "slightly flawed" or "joystick drift." Oh,. what's this?

Wow, such a cover is so classic. Rathalos and Nargacuga,. I gotta take this one home. Walking by here, I spotted a pachinko parlor. It's a pretty famous form of entertainment in Japan,. and I just happened to pass by today. Let's go in and see how it's played. But a word of sincere advice to all of you: don't gamble. Stepping into the store,. the first thing that hits you is how incredibly noisy it is. Actually, playing pachinko is pretty straightforward. You just put money into the machine,. and it spits out corresponding steel balls. You twist this knob here,. and the ball shoots out from one side of the machine. The harder you twist, the farther the ball goes. If the ball lands in the middle slot,. you win. In reality, while playing,. you're just holding onto this knob the whole time,. keeping it steady. There's no real skill or strategy involved. When you're done,. you can take all your remaining steel balls. to the front counter and exchange them for prizes. Here's the interesting part:. gambling is illegal in Japan,. so these pachinko parlors. can never exchange your steel balls. for cash. Instead,. they give you souvenirs like this. A silver token,. which isn't worth much on its own. But around the shop,. maybe within a 50-meter radius,. you can sell it for way more than its actual value. Strictly speaking,. the pachinko parlor isn't really running a gambling operation. They're just selling you a regular souvenir. But since this souvenir can be easily exchanged for cash,. everyone knows deep down that. it's essentially gambling. On the last night,. I decided to check out the Monster Hunter tavern up in Akihabara. There's actually an FF café. in the same building too. I'm still holding onto these cards I got here the year before last. Let's head upstairs. Ding ding. Here we are. This is a Stamp Card. I got two years ago. If you collect 10 stamps,. you can redeem an acrylic stand like this one. If one stamp only gets you a small card like this,. I'd definitely save up more stamps. First off, everyone gets a blind - box item. What kind of dragon you'll get on this coaster? It's all up to random chance. This manual here. is called. "The Beginner's Guide to the Monster Hunter Bar." And here's. the interesting part:. when you check out, they charge each person a table fee,.

but in return,. you get a daily login reward. Just like in the game,. where you get a reward for logging in every day. To sum up my experience at this Monster Hunter tavern today,. even if you bring a friend who doesn't play Monster Hunter,. it's still an absolutely fantastic time. The atmosphere is amazing. The bar's decor and the dishes they serve. all get updated when a new game in the series is released. This is also the first time I've ordered so many dishes here. Well, I guess you're mostly paying for. the names and the cool designs of the food. The prices aren't outrageously overpriced because of the IP,. but they're still a bit on the pricey side. When "Monster Hunter: World" came out,. I went to the Shinjuku branch. Then, for "Rise" and now for "Wilds," I've come to the Akihabara store. As soon as you walk in, there's a Palico greeting you at the door. Inside, you can see the quests. on the quest board. There's a Rathalos greatsword hanging on the wall,. an Iceborne bow,. and up in the sky, there's a representation of a Yian Kut - Ku hunting ground. It just feels like the Yian Kut - Ku is about to charge and spit fireballs at you. After my visit five years ago,. I was so inspired that. I started buying 1:1 scale weapons from the game. to decorate my studio. So, this trip to Japan has come to an end. But this time, I approached it with an observational mindset,. and it allowed me to discover many things. that I hadn't noticed during previous travels. Before setting off,. I was actually curious about. why Japan always gives us this stereotype of. being stuck in the past. Well, after coming to Tokyo this time,. I've got some new thoughts. More than one-tenth of Japan's population is in Tokyo. After long-term development,. there are so many different rules here. Everyone tries their best to go about their business without disturbing others,. following the most reliable procedures. Nowadays, in Japanese ramen shops,. many still use coin-operated ticket vending machines. And a lot of companies are still using fax machines. But perhaps it's because there are just too many constraints. that there's a lack of fertile ground for something new and innovative to emerge. In the past, I used to think that. if I were a tech and gadget blogger,. I'd have to travel abroad frequently. since the latest and greatest products are always released overseas. But in recent years,. I've noticed that more and more fun stuff is being launched in China first. At that point,. actually, many friends from all over the world. come to watch our videos. to get the latest news right away. That's why my YouTube videos. have always had meticulously proofread English subtitles. Sometimes, seeing the interactions from international viewers. is quite interesting. Oh, and here's something fun.

I've managed to secure my Schengen visa, UK visa, and US visa. So, if in the future, there's anything interesting. you guys want to see,. I can just fly over there and take you all along for the ride.

=============

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zfvLfK1HUPQ

歡迎訂閱切狸~ 在這裡你會看到很多有趣的數碼資訊哦！ | Please don’t forget to subscribe 💋：https://bit.ly/3jQ3429

-------------------------------------------------- ----------------------------------

其實對於日本，除了實體購物和網路購買的差別，我有很多好奇的地方，像是傳說中的日本古老的電腦、手機，是不是真的很多人在用？又例如國產的華米OV在國外的版本，跟國銀用起來有沒有什麼差別？如果大家想看的話，下次再帶大家來一次日本。除此之外，也可以再在評論或彈幕裡留下你們的建議，我之後可以直接飛過去帶大家一起康！

F O L L O W M E

Weibo: @大狸子切切里

Bilibili: https://space.bilibili.com/471303350
